title: Make non-trigger movie joystick use the exact existing single skip action
summary: 0.6.58 stops trying to tune movie scrub math and makes non-trigger movie joystick fire the exact existing 15-second skip action once per stick excursion, while leaving image stepping and trigger-modified video behavior alone.
changes: Non-trigger movie joystick input now dispatches the exact existing Player.SkipForward or Player.SkipBackward action once per stick excursion.
The movie path no longer accumulates custom target math or repeats while the stick is held in one direction.
Image stepping and trigger-modified video next or previous behavior remain unchanged.
reasoning: The operator explicitly wants movie joystick to match the existing skip button behavior exactly, as a single action rather than a held repeat. This slice keeps the blast radius to the non-trigger movie joystick branch only.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: A right movie joystick nudge should match one 15-second skip forward action.
A left movie joystick nudge should match one 15-second skip backward action.
Holding the stick should not repeat more skips until it returns through neutral.
knownIssues: This slice does not change image stepping behavior.
This slice does not change trigger-modified video next and previous behavior.
This slice does not change slideshow, right-stick volume, or deferred gaze work.
verification: The non-trigger movie joystick branch in FASyncRuntime.CuaPlayerInputModule.cs calls TrySkipForwardStandalonePlayer or TrySkipBackwardStandalonePlayer exactly once per stick excursion.
The non-trigger movie joystick branch no longer uses custom normalized target accumulation.
